fields.description:
# S7 — Documentation, ADR, and regenerated references for MCP auth

## Why
The capability must be usable and auditable by a third-party operator, and the design decisions +
residual risks must be recorded for maintainers and the rigorous security review. This story writes
the operator guide and the ADR, and does the FINAL consistency check of the derived reference
artifacts (each key-adding story S1–S6 already registers ITS keys and regenerates its slice; S7
confirms the integrated whole is consistent).

## What
- New `docs/mcp-auth.md` — the operator guide:
  - How to enable the HTTP transport + auth; each of the five verifier modes (`static`, `jwt`,
    `introspection`, `proxy`, `custom`) with a flat-config example each, and the closed
    `auth_strategies` vocabulary. State the flat-schema limitation: **one instance per strategy type**
    (for multiple issuers, use the `custom` verifier).
  - The **security model:** resource-server-only (no AS/DCR); the composite as the single audience/
    fail-closed choke point (every token's `resource` == `auth_resource_server_url`, RFC 8707); no
    token passthrough; DNS-rebinding/Origin defaults; the non-loopback bind requirements (allowlists
    + TLS-at-edge ack); tokens/secrets never logged.
  - **Behind-a-proxy deployment:** TLS terminates at the edge (nginx/ALB) and rebar binds loopback;
    the external host must be in `http_allowed_hosts` and the external origin in `http_allowed_origins`;
    `auth_resource_server_url` MUST be the external https URL (it drives the served PRM `resource` and
    the `WWW-Authenticate: resource_metadata` URL — NOT the request scheme), and is validated https
    for non-loopback. Include a concrete nginx/ALB header + allowlist example.
  - **The three interactive-client connection paths** (so RS-only isn't experienced as "can't
    connect"): (1) static bearer via Claude Code `--header "Authorization: Bearer …"` / Claude.ai
    `static_headers` — pairs with the static verifier; (2) automatic OAuth when the org IdP supports
    DCR or CIMD (Keycloak/Auth0/Okta can); (3) pre-registered `--client-id`/`--client-secret` for
    IdPs without anonymous DCR (Entra, Cognito). Note rebar serves the PRM that drives discovery.
  - **Residual risks (documented, not fixed here):** authenticated ≠ authorized — any authenticated
    principal wields rebar's full ambient authority (writes auto-push; `reconcile live` uses rebar's
    own creds) gated only by server-level `required_scopes` and the existing `REBAR_MCP_READONLY`/
    `ALLOW_JIRA_SYNC`/`ALLOW_LLM` env gates, which now also gate remote actors; per-tool
    scope→capability authorization is deferred; rate-limiting/brute-force is delegated to the edge
    proxy in v1, with mandated ≥128-bit static tokens (document the generator one-liner).
- A new ADR under `docs/adr/` recording: the resource-server-only decision (no AS / no DCR); building
  our own verifiers on the official SDK seam vs adopting standalone `fastmcp` (semver-exempt auth,
  CVE history); the composite audience choke point (motivated by the SDK having no `.resource`
  backstop + the audience-confusion precedent PVE-2026-93393); the five verifier modes; the transport
  hardening (citing GHSA-9h52-p55h-vw2f DNS-rebinding, oauth2-proxy CVE-2026-40575 forwarded-header,
  CVE-2026-27124 confused-deputy); and the explicitly deferred items (AS/DCR, per-tool authz,
  in-process rate-limiting, AWS deployment).
- **Final reference/manifest consistency check:** confirm `docs/mcp-reference.md`/`docs/env-vars.md`
  are fully regenerated for the integrated set of new keys and `scripts/check_server_manifest.py` +
  the reference drift gates pass on the combined branch (S1–S6 each regenerated their slice; S7
  verifies the union).

## Approach & rationale
Documentation-and-ADR story sequenced last so it documents the *actually shipped* surface (S1–S6);
regeneration (not hand-editing) keeps the derived docs authoritative and drift-gated. A docs/mechanical
leaf — no runtime behavior.

## Scope
`docs/mcp-auth.md`, the ADR, and the final reference/manifest consistency verification only. No code
behavior. Subset of the epic. Depends on S1–S6 (documents them).

## Acceptance Criteria
The ADR filename is fixed at `docs/adr/0050-mcp-optional-auth-resource-server.md`; each criterion
below has a single, unambiguous shell proving command that exits 0.

- [ ] `docs/mcp-auth.md` exists and covers: enabling HTTP+auth; all five verifier modes with a
      flat-config example each + the `auth_strategies` vocabulary + the one-instance-per-type
      limitation; the security model (composite audience choke point, fail-closed, no passthrough);
      behind-a-proxy deployment (TLS + allowlists + `auth_resource_server_url` drives discovery URLs)
      with an nginx/ALB example; the three interactive-client connection paths; and the residual risks
      (per-tool authz deferral, rate-limiting delegation, static-token entropy). Proving command:
      `test -f docs/mcp-auth.md && for h in 'Enabling the HTTP transport' '### `static`' '### `jwt`' '### `introspection`' '### `proxy`' '### `custom`' 'single audience' 'Behind a proxy' 'interactive-client' 'out of scope'; do grep -Fq "$h" docs/mcp-auth.md || exit 1; done`
      (each required section string is present).
- [ ] `docs/mcp-auth.md` is linked from `docs/README.md`. Proving command:
      `grep -Fq 'mcp-auth.md' docs/README.md && python scripts/check_docs_index.py --check`
      (the docs-index completeness gate exits 0 with the new file linked).
- [ ] The ADR `docs/adr/0050-mcp-optional-auth-resource-server.md` exists and records: the
      resource-server-only + build-own-verifiers decision, the composite audience choke point, the five
      verifier modes, the transport-hardening vulnerability CLASSES it mitigates (DNS-rebinding,
      forwarded-header spoofing, confused-deputy, audience-confusion), and the deferred items. The
      advisory identifiers (GHSA-9h52-p55h-vw2f, CVE-2026-40575, CVE-2026-27124, PVE-2026-93393) are
      cited as upstream-tracked references for those classes, NOT asserted as independently
      NVD-verified facts. Proving command:
      `test -f docs/adr/0050-mcp-optional-auth-resource-server.md && for s in 'esource' 'composite' 'DNS-rebinding' 'forwarded-header' 'confused-deputy' 'audience' 'defer'; do grep -Fiq "$s" docs/adr/0050-mcp-optional-auth-resource-server.md || exit 1; done`.
- [ ] `docs/mcp-reference.md` and `docs/env-vars.md` are regenerated for the integrated new-key set and
      the drift gates for them and for `server.json` pass. Proving command (all three exit 0):
      `python scripts/gen_mcp_reference.py --check && python scripts/gen_env_registry.py --check && python scripts/check_server_manifest.py`.
- [ ] `make lint && make test` pass (docs/mechanical leaf; no typecheck-relevant code added here).

## Compatibility
Docs only; no runtime or dependency change beyond what S1–S6 introduced.
